title: approvals — reminder channel driver specifications
description: The approval engine's `ApprovalReminderNotification` delivers via one of the notifications module's registered channels — mail, in_app, push, slack. This module doesn't ship its own reminder drivers; it delegates to the notifications module's DriverManager. This file documents the CONTRACT the notifications module's channels must honour to serve reminder dispatch correctly, and any approvals-specific channel resolution logic.

contract.note: The channels themselves live in the notifications module. Approvals only stipulates the shape of the notification payload + the resolution priority.

channel_resolution.note: How approvals picks channels for the reminder dispatch. Consulted in DispatchApprovalRemindersJob AND for manual dispatches.
priority: 2) Tenant's approvals.default_reminder_channels setting (rare — tenants usually leave this at default)

drivers.name: mail
drivers.usage: Primary reminder channel — most human approvers rely on inbox notifications.
drivers.notes: Digest-mode users bundle N reminders into a single digest email dispatched daily.

drivers.name: in_app
drivers.usage: Secondary — surfaces in the SPA notification-bell dropdown + the pending-my-review inbox.

drivers.name: push
drivers.usage: Mobile app users. Opt-in per user_settings.
drivers.notes: Push payload is minimal (action label + expires_at). Tapping opens the mobile app to the approval detail view.

drivers.name: slack
drivers.usage: Team-approval flows where approvers work in a shared Slack channel. Opt-in via tenant integration + per-user linking.
drivers.notes: Slack-native buttons on the message let approvers approve/reject inline WITHOUT leaving Slack — the button POSTs to the tenant plane approve/reject endpoint via Slack's slash-command bridge. Deferred to Wave 2.

notes: The reminder cadence is APPROVAL-owned (template.reminder_config); the channel routing is NOTIFICATIONS-owned. Two axes, one dispatch.
ApprovalRequestedNotification uses the same channel-resolution path — reminders are just repeated dispatches with an incrementing reminder_number.

If a channel dispatch fails, the notifications module's retry policy applies (exponential backoff, tries=3). Approvals does not orchestrate per-channel retry — that's the notifications module's contract.
